title: Seed K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml from operator
summary:
Security review of CC-0114 (move K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml seed from deploy-infra shell into the c5c3-operator). All focus areas are clean. The admin password is read from a Secret, materialised only into Secrets, and pushed via ESO PushSecret; it is never logged and never placed in a process argument — a genuine improvement over the retired in-pod `sh -c` shell seed. buildPasswordCloudsYAML embeds the password via Go %q, which double-quotes/escapes newlines and quotes and thus prevents YAML breakout (the old shell seed used an unquoted heredoc). Owner references are same-namespace only (childNamespace(cp)==cp.Namespace, SetControllerReference). The per-CR remoteRef.key (adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor: openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/admin/app-credential) is scoped by both namespace AND name, so one ControlPlane cannot read another's OpenBao path. Removing the shell mark_eso_managed for the app-credential path is correct: the path is no longer shell-pre-seeded, so it starts non-existent and ESO creates+stamps its own managed-by marker on first push (no guard collision). No new RBAC is added; the existing external-secrets.io/externalsecrets marker (controlplane_controller.go:82) and Helm ClusterRole (_helpers.tpl:199) already cover the esov1 ExternalSecret, and esov1 is registered in main.go:51.

CC-0114 moves the K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml seed from a deploy-infra shell script into the c5c3-operator via three new reconcileKORC steps (seedBootstrapCloudsYAML, EnsurePushSecret, ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret). From a code-quality/architecture standpoint the change is clean: it reuses buildPasswordCloudsYAML and appCredCloudsYAMLKey (DRY), all sub-resource names derive from cp.Name (multi-instance safe), the three steps are idempotent/no-churn on steady state (proven by tests including IdempotentNoChurn), the seed is correctly write-if-empty with a re-mint repopulate path, and the new code matches the sibling CreateOrUpdate + inline-Reason-string style already used by ensureAdminPasswordCloud/ensureAppCredentialSecret. The placement DECISION comment is sound (it correctly identifies the issue's two stated constraints as mutually inconsistent given the real call order ensureKORCAdminImports@423 before ensureAppCredentialSecret@450, and intra-pass ordering does not affect async K-ORC convergence), and the esov1 (ExternalSecret, GA) vs esov1alpha1 (PushSecret) split is intentional and documented. No defects found in changed files.

Testing review of CC-0114 (operator-owned K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml seed). Every acceptance criterion has a passing, meaningful test: ES shape+ownerRef+per-CR remoteRef, write-if-empty/non-overwrite/repopulate-after-remint, rendered-document parsing (yaml.Unmarshal of the SEEDED bytes, not the format-string input) asserting endpoint_type:internal and a genuinely-distinct projected auth_url for both default and non-default CRs, the reworked deadlock test (old 'no push' assertion removed; now asserts seed+PushSecret+ES are created), the two edge cases (defer-before-seed asserts ABSENCE of PushSecret+ES; steady-state asserts a stable ResourceVersion against a fixture where both ensureAppCredentialSecret and the seed are provably no-ops), and the integration test which now waits for (never pre-creates) the operator-rendered ES before SimulateExternalSecretSync across both the full and multi-CP paths. All 18 CC-0114 unit subtests pass. Index-based container assertions are uniformly guarded by HaveLen(1), so they are not brittle. One MINOR finding: TestReconcileKORC_FreshClusterSeedsPasswordCloudsYaml is a near-duplicate of TestReconcileKORC_FreshClusterSeedsCloudsYamlAndCreatesPushSecretAndExternalSecret (same reconcileKORC fixture/path; only unique coverage is the 'username:' substring) and is a consolidation candidate. No correctness or coverage gaps.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests assert seedBootstrapCloudsYAML renders a password-based clouds.yaml (contains 'password:') write-if-empty and re-seeds after key deletion
Integration tests assert the operator (not the test) creates the ES, with correct per-CR remoteRef.key, single Data entry, and ControlPlane controller-ownership
Test asserts no PushSecret/ExternalSecret is created before the admin password Secret exists (defer path)
Unit tests cover seedBootstrapCloudsYAML write-if-empty, no-overwrite-minted, re-mint repopulate
Unit tests cover ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret shape, owner ref, per-CR remote key, idempotent no-churn
Reworked deadlock test asserts operator now creates PushSecret + ExternalSecret on fresh cluster
Integration test asserts operator creates the ES (shape/owner) instead of pre-creating it
Rendered clouds.yaml document parse + endpoint_type-not-interface tests retained for the seed path

ES shape+ownerRef+per-CR remoteRef.key covered (ShapeAndOwnerRef asserts ClusterSecretStore/openBaoClusterStoreName, Target.Name, CreatePolicyOwner, single Data, SecretKey=clouds.yaml, RemoteRef.Key=adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor, Property=clouds.yaml, controller ownerRef Kind=ControlPlane *Controller=true)
Per-CR remoteRef tracks arbitrary name/namespace (PerCRRemoteKeyForNonDefaultName asserts literal openstack/keystone/tenant-a/controlplane-a/admin/app-credential)
ES idempotency proven via ResourceVersion stability across two passes (IdempotentNoChurn)
write-if-empty seed covered (WritesWhenCloudsYamlKeyEmpty; also asserts the 'value' key is preserved byte-for-byte)
Non-overwrite of minted clouds.yaml covered (DoesNotOverwriteMintedCloudsYaml asserts byte-for-byte equality with the pre-set minted document)
Repopulate-after-remint covered (RepopulatesAfterReMintDeletesKey, post-remint state = only 'value' present)

Rendered clouds.yaml is PARSED not input-asserted (seedAndReadCloudsYAML reads the Secret then yaml.Unmarshal; asserts endpoint_type:internal and auth_url) for BOTH default and non-default CR with genuinely-distinct projected auth_urls (controlplane-keystone.openstack vs cp-keystone.default), cross-checked against keystoneEndpointURL(cp)
endpoint_type-key-not-interface boundary covered (UsesEndpointTypeKeyNotInterface asserts HaveKey endpoint_type, NotTo HaveKey interface)
Reworked deadlock test drives reconcileKORC (not reconcileAdminCredential) and asserts seed+PushSecret+ES all created; old IsNotFound 'no push' assertion is removed
Edge: DefersBeforeSeedWhenAdminPasswordMissing asserts WaitingForAdminPassword AND absence (IsNotFound) of both PushSecret and ExternalSecret

Edge: SteadyStateDoesNotOverwriteMintedCloudsYaml asserts RV stable on a fixture where ensureAppCredentialSecret (value set, ownerRef set) and seed (clouds.yaml set) are both verifiably no-ops, so a non-zero RV delta could only be a seed regression — not vacuous

Integration test no longer pre-creates the ES (manual c.Create removed); asserts operator creates it via Eventually+Get, validates Data len/SecretKey/RemoteRef.Key/Property and controller ownerRef, then SimulateExternalSecretSync; applied to both FullReconcile and multi-CP driver
Index-based container assertions (Spec.Data[0], SecretStoreRefs[0]) are all preceded by HaveLen(1) guards — not brittle
Old test name fully removed (no dangling FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush reference outside the replacement comment)
PushSecret remoteRef target asserted to match per-CR path and openBaoClusterStoreName (PushSecretRemoteKeyMatchesPerCRPath)
All 18 CC-0114 unit subtests pass locally

code_quality_checklist.item: Each new step in reconcileKORC guarded with a distinct KORCReady=False Reason (SeedCloudsYamlError/PushSecretError/ExternalSecretError)
Error wraps use %w and reference object name, not secret contents
buildPasswordCloudsYAML reused by seed (no duplicated YAML template)
appCredCloudsYAMLKey constant reused for Secret key, ES SecretKey, and RemoteRef Property
New code matches sibling CreateOrUpdate + inline-Reason-string style (no gratuitous new pattern)
Error wrapping with %w and contextual messages on every new path
Placement DECISION and esov1-vs-esov1alpha1 rationale documented in comments
Test helpers (seedAndReadCloudsYAML, defaultNamedControlPlane) use t.Helper() and read the rendered Secret, not the input format string
Tests do not rely on implicit defaults (SecretName explicitly 'k-orc-clouds-yaml' on the CR; non-default test sets distinct name+namespace+UID)
Test names accurately describe their scope

security_checklist.item: Admin password never logged (all log/error messages use %v of an error or static strings; no password interpolation)
Admin password never passed as a process/command-line argument (rendered in-process to []byte, written to a Secret)
YAML injection prevented: password embedded via Go %q (double-quoted, escapes newlines/quotes); improvement over old unquoted shell heredoc
No cross-namespace owner references: all owned objects in childNamespace(cp)==cp.Namespace, SetControllerReference is same-namespace
Per-CR OpenBao path scoped by namespace AND name (adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor) — one ControlPlane cannot read another's credential path
ESO managed-by: removal of shell mark_eso_managed for app-credential path is correct (path no longer pre-seeded; ESO PushSecret creates+marks it itself)
No new RBAC markers added; existing externalsecrets RBAC marker and Helm ClusterRole already cover esov1 ExternalSecret (same external-secrets.io group)
Write-if-empty seed never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml and never touches the 'value' key
Password embedded via buildPasswordCloudsYAML (%q) and only ever materialised into a Secret / pushed via ESO — no host process-arg exposure (improvement over deleted shell seed)
SetControllerReference set on seeded Secret and created ExternalSecret for GC and ownership
Write-if-empty guard prevents clobbering a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
No new test introduces a plaintext credential into a host process arg; password materialised into a Secret only

architecture_checklist.item: Seed/Push/ES steps placed after ensureAppCredentialSecret and before the AC re-mint decision; ordering documented and convergence-safe (K-ORC retries auth async)
ExternalSecret is esov1; PushSecret stays esov1alpha1 (intentional, documented)
Placement after ensureAppCredentialSecret, before re-mint decision — sound given real call order and async K-ORC retry
ExternalSecret created in childNamespace(cp) co-located with K-ORC CRs (CloudCredentialsRef resolved in resource namespace)
ExternalSecret name derives from Spec CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName with korcCloudsYamlSecretName fallback
esov1 (GA ExternalSecret) vs esov1alpha1 (PushSecret) split correct and registered in main.go scheme
Static single-identity ESO manifest retired; orc-system copy retained; shell seed fully removed
Tests assert the ES type is esov1 (external-secrets.io/v1) and PushSecret stays esov1alpha1

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Per-CR path formula centralised in adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor and reused by both PushSecret writer and ExternalSecret reader
No duplicated clouds.yaml builder — single buildPasswordCloudsYAML
Double CreateOrUpdate over the same Secret is justified by SRP (value vs clouds.yaml) + distinct inputs; not premature consolidation
TestReconcileKORC_FreshClusterSeedsPasswordCloudsYaml overlaps heavily with the reworked deadlock test (same fixture/path); flagged as a consolidation candidate (MINOR)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Missing admin password defers with WaitingForAdminPassword BEFORE any seed/push/ES create (verified by TestReconcileKORC_DefersBeforeSeedWhenAdminPasswordMissing)
Each new step returns ctrl.Result{} + err with a distinct condition Reason on failure
No swallowed errors in the three new steps

Each seed step in reconcileKORC carries a distinct KORCReady=False Reason (SeedCloudsYamlError/PushSecretError/ExternalSecretError); the defer-before-seed edge case proves the early WaitingForAdminPassword return

defensive_checklist.item: secret.Data nil-guarded before write; SecretName falls back to korcCloudsYamlSecretName on empty spec (webhook-bypass edge)
Empty SecretName falls back to korcCloudsYamlSecretName constant
Steady-state passes are no-ops (write-if-empty + spec-diff CreateOrUpdate) — no churn / no consumer wakeups
Absence-of-old-state asserted in mutation/defer tests (no PushSecret/ES before password; no application_credential_id in fresh seed; minted doc unchanged)

issues_found.description: TestReconcileKORC_FreshClusterSeedsPasswordCloudsYaml is a near-duplicate of TestReconcileKORC_FreshClusterSeedsCloudsYamlAndCreatesPushSecretAndExternalSecret. Both build the identical fresh-cluster fixture (cp + adminPasswordSecret), drive reconcileKORC, and assert the seeded clouds.yaml is the password document (ContainSubstring password:, NotTo ContainSubstring application_credential_id). The only coverage unique to the standalone test is the extra ContainSubstring("username:") assertion, which could fold into the reworked deadlock test, removing the duplicate.

suggested_improvements: Optional hardening note (not a defect): Go %q and YAML double-quoted-scalar escape grammars diverge for some non-printable/control characters (e.g. certain \xHH/\uHHHH forms). For the current threat model this is moot because the admin password originates from a controlled Secret and is a hex string in practice, but if AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef could ever carry arbitrary bytes, consider rendering clouds.yaml via a YAML marshaller (e.g. sigs.k8s.io/yaml) instead of fmt.Sprintf+%q to guarantee escape-grammar correctness for all byte values.

On an upgraded cluster a pre-CC-0114 deploy may have already run `bao kv put` on the per-CR app-credential path; since the operator's PushSecret already targeted it before this change, it should already be ESO-managed, so no migration action is required — worth a one-line note in the upgrade docs for operators reasoning about the managed-by marker.

OPTIONAL (no action required): seedBootstrapCloudsYAML and ensureAppCredentialSecret each run a separate Get+Update CreateOrUpdate over the same {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret on the fresh-cluster path. This is correct and SRP-clean as written (distinct keys, distinct inputs, distinct failure Reasons), and is a no-op on steady state. If a future change ever wants to shave one round-trip, the two could be merged into a single CreateOrUpdate mutator — but doing so would require threading password into ensureAppCredentialSecret and merging two responsibilities, so the current separation is the better trade-off and should be kept unless a measured hot-path cost appears.

OPTIONAL (repo-wide, out of scope): the external-feedback 'extract repeated condition strings into typed constants' pattern is not applied here, but the new inline Reason strings correctly match the existing convention throughout reconcile_korc.go (SecretError, AdminImportError, KORCCRDNotInstalled, ...). Extracting Reasons into typed constants would be a separate repo-wide cleanup, not a CC-0114 concern.

Optional: in TestSeedBootstrapCloudsYAML_RenderedDocumentParsesWithInternalEndpointAndProjectedAuthURL, also assert the parsed auth.username/password equal the projected korcAdminUsername/testAdminPassword to lock the credential projection alongside the endpoint projection (currently those are only covered by ContainSubstring checks in sibling tests).

detected_patterns.name: controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate + SetControllerReference for owned sub-resources (matches sibling ensureAdminPasswordCloud/ensureAppCredentialSecret)
write-if-empty idempotency guard for seeded Secret keys
per-CR resource naming derived from cp.Name / Spec refs for multi-instance safety
esov1 GA ExternalSecret vs esov1alpha1 PushSecret API-version split
Reject vacuous test assertions behind conditional guards: none found
Assert absence of old state in mutation tests: satisfied (DefersBeforeSeed IsNotFound, SteadyState RV stable, no application_credential_id)
Avoid brittle index-based container assertions: satisfied (all [0] indexes guarded by HaveLen(1))
Tests must not rely on implicit default values: satisfied (SecretName + non-default name/ns set explicitly)
Ensure test names accurately describe coverage scope: satisfied
Consolidate near-duplicate tests: one candidate flagged (MINOR)
Parse rendered output not input: satisfied (yaml.Unmarshal of seeded Secret bytes)
